underlying: TSA daily passenger throughput, US checkpoints, 2026-04-28

recent_observations.note: FOIA PDF (4/19-4/25); deepest Tue dip of month

method: Anchored on real TSA hourly throughput PDFs (3/29-4/25). 17:30 UTC refresh: TSA FOIA reading room not directly fetchable (tsa.gov returns 403 to scraping; daily passenger-volumes page reportedly offline during shutdown), and 4/26-onward weekly PDF not yet found via Google index — typical FOIA cadence publishes Wed/Thu, so 4/26-5/2 PDF unlikely before 4/29-4/30. No new daily anchor data. News scan: DHS shutdown REMAINS ACTIVE per NPR 4/27 (Congress under pressure as it returns to session) and Wikipedia (still ongoing). House passed a one-week stopgap 4/26 (visahq), Senate has skinny plan, but no signed law as of refresh time — no whipsaw event. Most-likely Tue 4/28 ~ 1.55-1.65M: late-week 4/23-4/25 already showed late-Apr WoW deltas of only -2 to -4% (not -15% like Tue 4/21), and TSA absenteeism dropped ~43-45% post Trump back-pay order. Nudged nowcast up 20K to 1.58M on stabilization signal; tightened sigma from 160K to 140K (no FOIA confirm but no contradicting data either, and the late-week WoW pattern reduces left-tail risk).
notes: Refresh at 17:30 UTC: anchor 1.56M -> 1.58M, sigma 160K -> 140K, confidence MEDIUM (unchanged). Drivers: (1) No new FOIA PDF accessible — tsa.gov returns 403 to scraping and the live passenger-volumes page is reportedly offline due to shutdown; the 4/26-5/2 weekly PDF would normally drop Wed/Thu so absent data is consistent with timing rather than a signal. (2) Shutdown still active per NPR 4/27 and Wikipedia — House passed a one-week DHS stopgap on 4/26 but no signed law yet, so no upside whipsaw. (3) TSA absenteeism already dropped ~43-45% earlier in April after Trump's back-pay order (reflected in 4/23-4/25 stabilization). (4) No same-day delay anomaly news for 4/28 specifically beyond ongoing background pressure. Lowest strike (2.07M) sits ~3.5 sigma above 1.58M mean — every YES floors at 0.005. Mid-quote 0.225 on T2570000 remains far above model fair (~0). The biggest residual risk is (a) a surprise shutdown-end signing late today that triggers immediate 4/28 absenteeism normalization (would push closer to pre-shutdown Tue ~2.0M), or (b) a true 4/26-onward PDF leak. Neither has occurred in this refresh.
